---
title: "Final Project"
author: "Madison Rogers"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
head(beaver)
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
# Load data
beaver <- read.csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv")
elk <- read.csv("corrected_elk_counts_09Jul2023_Park_only.csv")
fall <- read.csv("fall_height_data_2001_2020.csv")
wolf <- read.csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv")

```

```{r}
library(readr)
wolf <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv")
```

```{r}
library(readr)

wolf <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv")

```

```{r}
list.files()
```

```{r}
ls()
```

```{r}
dim(beaver)
dim(elk)
dim(fall)
dim(wolf)
head(fall)
```

```{r}
head(wolf)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(readr)

beaver <- read_csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv") |> clean_names()
elk    <- read_csv("corrected_elk_counts_09Jul2023_Park_only.csv") |> clean_names()
fall   <- read_csv("fall_height_data_2001_2020.csv") |> clean_names()
wolf   <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv") |> clean_names()
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(readr)
beaver <- read_csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))

elk <- read_csv("corrected_elk_counts_09Jul2023_Park_only.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))

fall <- read_csv("fall_height_data_2001_2020.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))

wolf <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))
```

```{r}
beaver <- read_csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-x1)

View(beaver)
```

```{r}
beaver <- read_csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of("...1"))
```

```{r}
beaver <- read_csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |> clean_names() |> select(-any_of("...1"))
elk    <- read_csv("corrected_elk_counts_09Jul2023_Park_only.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |> clean_names() |> select(-any_of("...1"))
fall   <- read_csv("fall_height_data_2001_2020.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |> clean_names() |> select(-any_of("...1"))
wolf   <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |> clean_names() |> select(-any_of("...1"))
```

```{r}
names(beaver)
```

```{r}
beaver <- read_csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of("x1"))
```

```{r}
names(beaver)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(readr)

beaver <- read_csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of(c("x1", "...1")))

elk <- read_csv("corrected_elk_counts_09Jul2023_Park_only.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of(c("x1", "...1")))

fall <- read_csv("fall_height_data_2001_2020.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of(c("x1", "...1")))

wolf <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of(c("x1", "...1")))
```

```{r}
names(beaver)
names(elk)
names(fall)
names(wolf)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(readr)

beaver <- read_csv("beaver_site_height_data_2015_2019.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of(c("x1", "...1"))) |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))

elk <- read_csv("corrected_elk_counts_09Jul2023_Park_only.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of(c("x1", "...1"))) |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))

fall <- read_csv("fall_height_data_2001_2020.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of(c("x1", "...1"))) |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))

wolf <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  clean_names() |>
  select(-any_of(c("x1", "...1"))) |>
  distinct() |>
  mutate(across(where(is.character), str_trim))
```

```{r}
wolf_long <- wolf |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("x"),
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "wolf_population"
  ) |>
  mutate(year = as.numeric(str_remove(year, "^x")))
```

```{r}
beaver_yearly <- beaver |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    beaver_mean_height = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE),
    beaver_n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

fall_yearly <- fall |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    fall_mean_height = mean(fall_height, na.rm = TRUE),
    fall_n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

elk_yearly <- elk |>
  rename(
    elk_mean = mean,
    elk_lwr_cl = lwr_cl,
    elk_upr_cl = upr_cl
  )

wolf_yearly <- wolf_long |>
  group_by(year) |>
  summarize(
    wolf_mean_population = mean(wolf_population, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
combined_data <- beaver_yearly |>
  full_join(fall_yearly, by = "year") |>
  full_join(elk_yearly, by = "year") |>
  full_join(wolf_yearly, by = "year")
```

```{r}
glimpse(combined_data)
head(combined_data)
```

```{r}
colSums(is.na(combined_data))
```

```{r}
combined_data |>
  pivot_longer(-year) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20) +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free")
```

```{r}
cor_data <- combined_data |>
  select(-year) |>
  drop_na()

cor(cor_data)
```

```{r}
library(GGally)

ggpairs(cor_data)
```

```{r}
install.packages("GGally")
```

```{r}
library(GGally)
```

```{r}
install.packages("GGally", dependencies = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(GGally)
ggpairs(cor_data)
```

```{r}
cor.test(
  combined_data$wolf_mean_population,
  combined_data$elk_mean,
  use = "complete.obs"
)
```

```{r}
cor.test(
  combined_data$elk_mean,
  combined_data$fall_mean_height,
  use = "complete.obs"
)
```

```{r}
cor.test(
  combined_data$wolf_mean_population,
  combined_data$fall_mean_height,
  use = "complete.obs"
)
```

```{r}
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = wolf_mean_population, y = elk_mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Wolf vs Elk", x = "Wolf population", y = "Elk population")
```

```{r}
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = elk_mean, y = fall_mean_height)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Elk vs Vegetation", x = "Elk", y = "Plant height")
```

```{r}
ggplot(combined_data, aes(x = wolf_mean_population, y = fall_mean_height)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Wolf vs Vegetation", x = "Wolf", y = "Plant height")
```

```{r}
model <- lm(fall_mean_height ~ wolf_mean_population + elk_mean, data = combined_data)

summary(model)
```

```{r}
model2 <- lm(fall_mean_height ~ beaver_mean_height, data = combined_data)

summary(model2)
```

```{r}
combined_data |>
  pivot_longer(-year) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = value, color = name)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Trends over time")
```

```{r}
plot(model)
```

```{r}
combined_data |>
  pivot_longer(-year) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = name, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot()
```


***1) Assess population growth patterns of 3 individual species\
You can address questions like: Is population growth continuous or discrete? Ignoring interactions with other species, does it seem like population growth is exponential or logistic?***



```{r}
library(readr)

wolf_raw <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
names(wolf_raw)
head(wolf_raw)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# ---------------------------
# 1. Read files
# ---------------------------

wolf_raw <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
elk_raw  <- read_csv("corrected_elk_counts_09Jul2023_Park_only.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
tree_raw <- read_csv("fall_height_data_2001_2020.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# ---------------------------
# 2. Clean names
# ---------------------------

clean_names_simple <- function(df) {
  names(df) <- names(df) |>
    tolower() |>
    str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", "_") |>
    str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")
  df
}

wolf_raw <- clean_names_simple(wolf_raw)
elk_raw  <- clean_names_simple(elk_raw)
tree_raw <- clean_names_simple(tree_raw)

# ---------------------------
# 3. Show column names so we can see what exists
# ---------------------------

cat("WOLF COLUMNS:\n")
print(names(wolf_raw))
cat("\nELK COLUMNS:\n")
print(names(elk_raw))
cat("\nTREE COLUMNS:\n")
print(names(tree_raw))

cat("\nHEAD OF WOLF:\n")
print(head(wolf_raw))
cat("\nHEAD OF ELK:\n")
print(head(elk_raw))
cat("\nHEAD OF TREE:\n")
print(head(tree_raw))
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

# -----------------------------
# 1. Read raw files
# -----------------------------
wolf_raw <- read_csv("wolf_1995_2022_population_ynp_nr_nnrr.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
elk_raw  <- read_csv("corrected_elk_counts_09Jul2023_Park_only.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
tree_raw <- read_csv("fall_height_data_2001_2020.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Clean column names
# -----------------------------
names(wolf_raw) <- names(wolf_raw) |> tolower() |> str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", "_") |> str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")
names(elk_raw)  <- names(elk_raw)  |> tolower() |> str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", "_") |> str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")
names(tree_raw) <- names(tree_raw) |> tolower() |> str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", "_") |> str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")

# -----------------------------
# 3. Clean wolves
#    Keep Yellowstone total row,
#    then pivot years into a year column
# -----------------------------
wolf_yearly <- wolf_raw %>%
  filter(str_detect(site, regex("yellowstone", ignore_case = TRUE))) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -site,
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "wolf_mean"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    year = as.integer(year),
    wolf_mean = as.numeric(wolf_mean)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(wolf_mean)) %>%
  arrange(year)

# -----------------------------
# 4. Clean elk
#    Use yearly mean counts
# -----------------------------
elk_yearly <- elk_raw %>%
  transmute(
    year = as.integer(year),
    elk_mean = as.numeric(mean),
    elk_lwrcl = as.numeric(lwrcl),
    elk_uprcl = as.numeric(uprcl)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year)) %>%
  arrange(year)

# -----------------------------
# 5. Clean trees
#    Summarize fall_height by year
# -----------------------------
tree_yearly <- tree_raw %>%
  transmute(
    year = as.integer(year),
    fall_height = as.numeric(fall_height)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(fall_height)) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    tree_mean = mean(fall_height, na.rm = TRUE),
    tree_sd = sd(fall_height, na.rm = TRUE),
    tree_n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(year)

# -----------------------------
# 6. Join them WITHOUT losing years
#    full_join keeps as many rows as possible
# -----------------------------
wolves_elk_trees_full <- full_join(wolf_yearly, elk_yearly, by = "year") %>%
  full_join(tree_yearly, by = "year") %>%
  arrange(year)

# -----------------------------
# 7. Optional analysis dataset:
#    only keep years where all 3 exist
# -----------------------------
wolves_elk_trees_complete <- wolves_elk_trees_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(wolf_mean), !is.na(elk_mean), !is.na(tree_mean)) %>%
  arrange(year)

# -----------------------------
# 8. Save both versions
# -----------------------------
write_csv(wolves_elk_trees_full, "wolves_elk_trees_full.csv")
write_csv(wolves_elk_trees_complete, "wolves_elk_trees_complete.csv")

# -----------------------------
# 9. Check results
# -----------------------------
cat("Rows in full dataset:", nrow(wolves_elk_trees_full), "\n")
cat("Rows in complete dataset:", nrow(wolves_elk_trees_complete), "\n\n")

print(wolf_yearly)
print(elk_yearly)
print(tree_yearly)
print(wolves_elk_trees_full)
print(wolves_elk_trees_complete)

# -----------------------------
# 10. Quick plots
# -----------------------------

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_full, aes(x = year, y = wolf_mean)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Wolf Population by Year", x = "Year", y = "Wolf Population")

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_full, aes(x = year, y = elk_mean)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Elk Population by Year", x = "Year", y = "Elk Mean Count")

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_full, aes(x = year, y = tree_mean)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Tree Height by Year", x = "Year", y = "Mean Fall Height")

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_complete, aes(x = wolf_mean, y = elk_mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Wolves vs Elk", x = "Wolf Population", y = "Elk Mean Count")

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_complete, aes(x = elk_mean, y = tree_mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Elk vs Trees", x = "Elk Mean Count", y = "Mean Tree Height")

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_complete, aes(x = wolf_mean, y = tree_mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Wolves vs Trees", x = "Wolf Population", y = "Mean Tree Height")
```

```{r}
nrow(wolves_elk_trees_full)
nrow(wolves_elk_trees_complete)
head(wolves_elk_trees_complete)
```

```{r}
wolves_elk_trees_full
```

```{r}
wolves_elk_trees_complete
```

```{r}
range(wolf_yearly$year, na.rm = TRUE)
range(elk_yearly$year, na.rm = TRUE)
range(tree_yearly$year, na.rm = TRUE)

wolf_yearly
elk_yearly
tree_yearly
```

```{r}
wolves_elk_trees_full %>%
  select(year, wolf_mean, elk_mean, tree_mean)
```

```{r}
ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_full, aes(x = year, y = wolf_mean)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point()

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_full, aes(x = year, y = elk_mean)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point()

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_full, aes(x = year, y = tree_mean)) +
  geom_line() + geom_point()
```

```{r}
ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_complete, aes(x = wolf_mean, y = elk_mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)

ggplot(wolves_elk_trees_complete, aes(x = elk_mean, y = tree_mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
```

```{r}
range(wolf_yearly$year, na.rm = TRUE)
range(elk_yearly$year, na.rm = TRUE)
range(tree_yearly$year, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
tree_yearly <- tree_raw %>%
  filter(!is.na(year), !is.na(fall_height)) %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(
    tree_mean = mean(fall_height, na.rm = TRUE),
    tree_sd = sd(fall_height, na.rm = TRUE),
    tree_n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(year)
```

```{r}
wolves_elk_trees_full <- full_join(wolf_yearly, elk_yearly, by = "year") %>%
  full_join(tree_yearly, by = "year") %>%
  arrange(year)

wolves_elk_trees_complete <- wolves_elk_trees_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(wolf_mean), !is.na(elk_mean), !is.na(tree_mean))
```

```{r}
range(tree_yearly$year)
nrow(wolves_elk_trees_complete)
```

```{r}
nrow(wolves_elk_trees_complete)
```

```{r}
wolves_elk_trees_complete
```

```{r}
write.csv(wolves_elk_trees_complete, "wolves_elk_trees_complete.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
